name: Eddie Jefferson
abstract: Eddie Jefferson (August 3, 1918 – May 9, 1979) was an American jazz vocalist and lyricist. He is credited as an innovator of vocalese, a musical style in which lyrics are set to an instrumental composition or solo.

concerts.label: Sarah Vaughan, Singer, with Betty Carter and Eddie Jefferson

about_quotes.speaker: Janis Siegel
about_quotes.evidence: We had originally commissioned Eddie Jefferson ... to write the lyric. And Eddie was murdered tragically. And after a time went by we asked his widow if he had left anything behind in his papers... and she said no.
about_quotes.doc_title: Janis Siegel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: If it hadn’t been for King Pleasure, for Eddie Jefferson... I would not have had the road sign to point me toward lyricizing orchestral versions of things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: I'm glad I got a chance to perform with him, though... But it was in Detroit where I sat in with him, same club.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Jefferson came with me... he traveled with me... I made him the manager... I had Eddie Jefferson and me.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Jefferson, the singer... who was a very good friend of mine.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Mondello
about_quotes.evidence: I actually picked that up, I stole it from a can of Budweiser... That’s Gates. You know Gates carries the torch you know of, you know all of the Eddie Jefferson going all the way back to Pops.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Mondello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 19, 2022
